PBKS-2019.name: KL Rahul
PBKS-2019.source: web search: KL Rahul was Kings XI Punjab's primary keeper in 2019 (top run-scorer that season, kept wicket); a 3-way catches tie with Agarwal/Miller made the automatic most-catches fallback unreliable here.

DC-2023.name: PD Salt
DC-2023.source: web search: Phil Salt was Delhi Capitals' primary designated wicketkeeper in IPL 2023 (with Manish Pandey as backup); neither led the squad in catches that season, so the automatic fallback would have picked a non-keeper.

KKR-2025.name: Q de Kock
KKR-2025.source: web search: KKR's 2025 squad listed both Quinton de Kock and Rahmanullah Gurbaz as wicketkeepers; de Kock had more catches between the two (3 vs 2) so picked as primary.

PBKS-2008.name: KC Sangakkara
PBKS-2008.source: web search: Kumar Sangakkara was Kings XI Punjab's primary wicketkeeper in the inaugural 2008 IPL season.

KKR-2009.name: BB McCullum
KKR-2009.source: web search: Brendon McCullum captained and kept wicket for Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2009.

SRH-2017.name: NV Ojha
SRH-2017.source: web search: Naman Ojha was SRH's primary wicketkeeper through 2014-17; the most-catches fallback wrongly picked David Warner (10 catches) over Ojha (6).

DC-2011.name: NV Ojha
DC-2011.source: web search: Naman Ojha was Delhi Daredevils' first-choice wicketkeeper in 2011; the most-catches fallback wrongly picked captain Virender Sehwag (9 catches) over Ojha (8).

CSK-2021.name: MS Dhoni
CSK-2021.source: MS Dhoni was CSK's wicketkeeper-captain in 2021 (title-winning comeback season); tied 13-13 catches with Ravindra Jadeja, and the fallback's tiebreak picked Jadeja instead.

CSK-2008.name: MS Dhoni
CSK-2008.source: MS Dhoni was CSK's wicketkeeper-captain from the franchise's inception in 2008; the most-catches fallback wrongly picked Suresh Raina (10 catches) over Dhoni (6).

SRH-2025.name: H Klaasen
SRH-2025.source: Heinrich Klaasen was SRH's primary wicketkeeper in 2025; the most-catches fallback wrongly picked Abhishek Sharma (8 catches) over Klaasen (7).
